raw:
Based on the search results provided, I can only partially address your request, as the results contain limited information about Oklahoma's senior benefit programs. Here is what I can confirm from the search results:

**Programs with Oklahoma-specific information:**

```json
[
  {
    "name": "SoonerCare",
    "what_it_does": "Oklahoma's Medicaid program that provides health care coverage. Serves children under 19, adults with children under 18, pregnant women, and people over 65 or with blindness or disabilities.[7]",
    "who_its_for": "People over 65, individuals with disabilities, and other qualifying populations[7]",
    "official_url": "okdhs.org",
    "benefit_value": "Health care coverage (specific benefit amounts not detailed in search results)"
  },
  {
    "name": "ADvantage Services",
    "what_it_does": "A home-and community-based services Medicaid waiver program that helps people stay at home and in the community instead of nursing homes. Services include case management, personal care, home-delivered meals, skilled nursing, and adult day health.[1]",
    "who_its_for": "People age 65+ or adults age 21-64 with physical or other disabilities who qualify financially for SoonerCare Medicaid and meet medical Level of Care criteria[1]",
    "official_url": "oklahoma.gov/okdhs/services/aging/pas.html",
    "benefit_value": "Varies by service type (specific amounts not detailed)"
  },
  {
    "name": "State Health Insurance Counseling Program (SHIP)",
    "what_it_does": "Provides free counseling and assistance with Medicare questions and enrollment. Part of Oklahoma's Medicare Assistance Program funded by the Administration for Community Living.[2]",
    "who_its_for": "Medicare beneficiaries and seniors with Medicare questions[2]",
    "official_url": "oid.ok.gov/consumers/information-for-seniors/",
    "benefit_value": "Free counseling services (contact: 800-763-2828)"
  },
  {
    "name": "Older Americans Act (Title III) Programs",
    "what_it_does": "Provides congregate and home-delivered meals, health promotion, nutrition education, in-home assistance, transportation, and caregiver support through Area Agencies on Aging.[4]",
    "who_its_for": "Seniors 60 and over, individuals with disabilities, and low-income individuals[4]",
    "official_url": "oklahoma.gov/okdhs/services/aging/titleiii.html",
    "benefit_value": "Meals meet one-third of recommended daily nutritional requirements; specific service amounts vary"
  },
  {
    "name": "Respite Voucher Program",
    "what_it_does": "Provides voucher payments to family caregivers so they can pay another person to care for their loved one and take a break from caregiving duties.[7]",
    "who_its_for": "Family caregivers of seniors and disabled individuals[7]",
    "official_url": "okdhs.org/services/dd/Pages/RVPp.aspx",
    "benefit_value": "Voucher amounts not specified in search results"
  },
  {
    "name": "Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)",
    "what_it_does": "Federal program providing monthly benefits to purchase food. Available to eligible low-income seniors.[7]",
    "who_its_for": "Low-income seniors and other qualifying individuals[7]",
    "official_url": "Not provided in search results",
    "benefit_value": "Monthly benefit amount (specific amounts not detailed)"
  },
  {
    "name": "Commodity Supplemental Food Program (CSFP)",
    "what_it_does": "Provides commodity food boxes to low-income seniors monthly, containing 16-20 items such as pasta, canned meats, fruits, vegetables, cheese, milk, and cereal.[7]",
    "who_its_for": "Low-income seniors[7]",
    "official_url": "regionalfoodbank.org/get-help/csfp-program",
    "benefit_value": "One box monthly with 16-20 food items"
  }
]
```

**Programs not covered in search results:** The search results do not contain specific information about PACE, Medicare Savings programs (QMB, SLMB, QI), LIHEAP, Weatherization, Senior Medicare Patrol fraud prevention details, Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP), legal aid for seniors, or the long-term care ombudsman program as administered in Oklahoma. To obtain complete information on all requested programs, contact the **Senior Info Line at 1-800-211-2116** or visit your local Area Agency on Aging.[7]
